response.justification: Candidate has strong geospatial domain experience (founded GISCollective, contributed to Green Map) and has built low-latency, high-throughput systems in DLang at Sociomantic, which is conceptually adjacent to the role. However, the required modern C++ experience is thin and dated (~6 months at National Instruments a decade ago plus a bachelor thesis), and there is no Rust experience. Recent work is primarily TypeScript/Node.js, which is a significant mismatch with a C++-primary routing tiling pipeline. Worth a screening call to probe systems-programming depth and willingness to ramp on C++/Rust.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS domain experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput backend experience (DLang, millions of req/sec at Sociomantic)
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, testing tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and ~10 years old; no Rust experience listed
Recent stack is TypeScript/Node/AWS, not systems-level pipeline work
No evidence of distributed systems / fault-tolerance work at routing-tile scale
